parentQuestion: Which of the following statements about connective tissue is FALSE?

scaffolds.q: Connective tissue contains lots of extracellular matrix (material between cells).
scaffolds.explain: Yes! This is a defining feature of connective tissue - abundant matrix with scattered cells.
Correct! Well-vascularized = good blood supply. Avascular = NO blood vessels.

scaffolds.q: MOST connective tissues have a good blood supply (are vascular).
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Bone, fat, and loose connective tissues all have blood vessels.

scaffolds.q: CARTILAGE is the exception - it is AVASCULAR (no blood vessels).
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Cartilage gets nutrients by diffusion, not by direct blood supply.

scaffolds.q: Because cartilage has no blood supply, it heals SLOWLY.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Blood delivers healing cells and nutrients. No blood = slow delivery = slow repair.

scaffolds.q: The statement 'connective tissue is highly vascular' is NOT always true.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Cartilage proves this statement false - it has NO blood vessels.

scaffolds.q: All connective tissues develop from mesenchyme (embryonic connective tissue).
scaffolds.explain: Yes! This IS true for all connective tissues - they share a common embryonic origin.

scaffolds.q: When looking for a FALSE statement, remember: cartilage is AVASCULAR.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! 'Connective tissue is highly vascular' is FALSE because cartilage has no blood supply.

scaffolds.q: The FALSE statement is 'Connective tissue is highly vascular' - cartilage proves this wrong.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! The other statements (abundant matrix, from mesenchyme, support/binding function) are all TRUE.
